LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PAUL SYCKEL'S MOTION FOR ORDER STRIKING UNTIMELY AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT AND DISMISSING CROSS ACTION FOR FAILURE TO AMEND CROSS-COMPLAINT AFTER DEMURRER WAS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AND, REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS DENIED. AT A MINIMUM, THE GOLDEN DECLARATION PROVIDES A BASIS FOR A FINDING OF EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. THE MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED FOR THE SAME REASON BUT THE COURT CAUTIONS DEFENSE COUNSEL TO CORRECTLY CITE THE LAW IN HIS DEALINGS WITH PLAINTIFF. COURT ORDERS, THAT IN ADDITION TO REGULAR SERVICE, BOTH SIDES ARE TO SERVE EACH OTHER BY EITHER ELECTRONIC MAIL OR FACSIMILE. MR. VAN SYCKLE IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

(Administrative fee) is heard this day. The OSC is re-set to 6/21/2007 at 10:30 AM, in Department 212. Proceedings reported by: Floyd L. Butler, CSR#2518. (212)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Resonse To Form Interrogatories And In The Alternative Response; For For Monetary Sanction;Separate Statement
Off Calendar Mar-07-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCE, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Paul Syckel's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part.
Casanova
2.6 (b)
Granted. Defendant is ordered to respond completely.
4.1
Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond.
12.1, 15.1(b)
Denied. The answer is otherwise sufficient.
16.1
Granted. Defendant is ordered to respond. Defendant may not answer by both stating that it lacks information and implying, without stating any facts in support thereof, that it contends the plaintiff was injured by his dog.
16.2 to 16.8
Granted. To the extent that the answer reserves the right to amend the response, it is ordered stricken. The response must be full and complete at the time it is made. If there is a need to amend, it must be made in compliance with CCP Section 2030.310.
Lucky Dog
4.1
Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond.
16.1 to 16.8
Granted. To the extent that the answer reserves the right to amend the response, it is ordered stricken. The response must be full and complete at the time it is made. If there is a need to amend, it must be made in compliance with CCP Section 2030.310.
Yan
4.1
Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond.
16.1 to 16.8
Granted. To the extent that the answer reserves the right to amend the response, it is ordered stricken. The response must be full and complete at the time it is made. If there is a need to amend, it must be made in compliance with CCP Section 2030.310.
The request for monetary sanctions is denied. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PAUL SYCKEL'S MOTION FOR ORDER STRIKING UNTIMELY AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT AND DISMISSING CROSS ACTION FOR FAILURE TO AMEND CROSS-COMPLAINT AFTER DEMURRER WAS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AND, REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE.
APPEARANCES: PAUL M. VAN SYCKEL, PRO PER (415.359.9808) APPEARING ON HIS OWN BEHALF. DAVID B. GOLDEN, ESQUIRE (GOLDEN & KOPCKE, 415.399.9994) APPEARING FOR CROSS-COMPLAINANT CASNOVA.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS DENIED. AT A MINIMUM, THE GOLDEN DECLARATION PROVIDES A BASIS FOR A FINDING OF EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. THE MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED FOR THE SAME REASON BUT THE COURT CAUTIONS DEFENSE COUNSEL TO CORRECTLY CITE THE LAW IN HIS DEALINGS WITH PLAINTIFF. COURT ORDERS, THAT IN ADDITION TO REGULAR SERVICE, BOTH SIDES ARE TO SERVE EACH OTHER BY EITHER ELECTRONIC MAIL OR FACSIMILE. MR. VAN SYCKLE IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/kd) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-06-2006 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT PAUL M. VAN SYCKEL'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT OF DJJ'S A LUCKY DOG LLC, MELISSA CASANOVA, AND BELINDA YAN.
APPEARANCE: Paul Van Syckel, 359-9808 pltf/x-deft & moving party in propria persona; David B. Golden, Esq. of Golden Kopcke, LLP 399-9994 for defts/x-complainants & opposing parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court sustained the demurrer with leave to amend as to causes of action 1, 2, and 4; sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action 3 and 5. (302/PHA/KD) Judge: Paul H. Alvarado, Reporter: Beldon Yee, CSR #2647
